mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction sector activity, which compounds into higher corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (win rate < 45%) or the interaction term shows zero marginal explanatory power.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and rising input costs, which compresses profit margins and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the mechanism shows no directional bias outside easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate cut exceeding 25 basis points in a single meeting signals aggressive monetary accommodation, immediately lowering discount rates and producing positive S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines > 0.25% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative or flat in > 50% of instances following a >25bps rate cut, or the signal shows no statistical edge over random walk.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between stagnant nonfarm payrolls growth and rising unemployment indicates labor market slack and weakening economic momentum, which suppresses earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns (win rate < 40%) or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly expands corporate revenue bases and improves equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates fragile supply chains and delayed economic normalization, triggering risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines in recovery regimes are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation is below 2%.
